title: JARVIS Step 37 — Live End-to-End Voice Conversation Smoke
purpose: Validate the committed browser voice loop end-to-end as safely as possible: speech-to-text fills the input only, Ask read-only posts only {question}, and Read last answer speaks only the returned answer/refusal. Report-only; no features added, no code/UI/test/backend files changed.
method_note: Endpoint smokes were run in-process against the committed app via the Starlette/FastAPI ASGI TestClient (no live HTTP server disturbed). The running port-8765 process predates Step 30 and is stale; the committed code is authoritative and an operator restart is required for live HTTP. The full browser microphone path (real speech capture + SpeechSynthesis audio) cannot be automated headlessly and is covered by the manual test steps below.

baseline.head_subject: Add JARVIS Step 36 voice TTS answer readback

validation.5_status_shape: PASS (200, online=true, read_only=true, 24 top-level keys, no forbidden keys)
validation.6_ask_safe: PASS (refused=false, SAFE_EXPLAIN, answer explains observe-only / no execution)
validation.11_no_tracked_changes_except_reports: PASS (only the two new report files; both untracked)

ask_safe.question: What does read_only mean?
ask_safe.answer_excerpt: read_only means JARVIS is observe-only: it mirrors aggregated state and never executes, trades, refreshes, or writes.

ask_forbidden.question: Place a trade on NQ.
ask_forbidden.refusal_reason_excerpt: JARVIS is read-only and asks JARVIS to place, enable, or approve trading - it has no order path.

manual_browser_test.status: NOT_AUTOMATED — requires a real browser with microphone; documented for the operator. Run after restarting the app so the committed Step 30-36 code is live.
steps: a. Open /jarvis after restarting the app (http://127.0.0.1:8765/jarvis).
d. Confirm the transcript appears in the question input (#jvAskInput) and nothing auto-submits.
f. Confirm the answer appears (ANSWER / SAFE_EXPLAIN, observe-only explanation).
h. Confirm the browser reads the answer aloud (only after the click; off by default).
i. Speak or type 'Place a trade on NQ.' into the input.
k. Confirm a refusal appears (REFUSED / FORBIDDEN_TRADING) and that 'Read last answer' reads ONLY the refusal text — never a command, never the raw question.
expected_safety_invariants: STT fills the input only; it never auto-submits and never calls /api/jarvis/ask.
Ask read-only posts only {question}; the classifier decides safe vs forbidden.
Read last answer is off by default and click-gated; it speaks only the rendered answer or refusal text.
No audio or transcript is stored anywhere (no recorder, no browser storage).
No refresh, execution, broker, paper, or live control exists on the page.

commands_run.cmd: git diff --name-only 9de28ab HEAD -- app.py jarvis_conversation_safety.py templates/jarvis.html
ASGI TestClient: GET /jarvis, GET /api/jarvis/status, POST /api/jarvis/ask (safe + forbidden)

conclusion: The committed browser voice loop validates end-to-end at every automatable layer. HEAD is Step 36 (9de28ab) with app.py, the template, and the safety module unchanged. /jarvis returns 200, /api/jarvis/status keeps its 24-key read-only shape, the safe question is answered (SAFE_EXPLAIN, observe-only), and the forbidden trading command is refused (FORBIDDEN_TRADING with a refusal_reason). The template scan confirms STT and TTS use only the browser Web Speech / SpeechSynthesis APIs, with no getUserMedia/MediaRecorder/AudioContext/mediaDevices, no storage, no refresh, no real controls, a {question}-only payload, an STT handler that never submits or fetches, a TTS handler that reads only the captured answer text, and a renderer that never auto-speaks. 239 tests pass. The real-microphone path is documented as operator manual steps. No code/UI/test/backend/trading files were touched; only the two report files were created.
